subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Core Components
- **Personal game boards** — 4 double-sided boards (1 per player), each showing a landscape with rocks, trees, and a river.
- **Building tiles** — Identical polyomino tile sets for each player (various shapes and sizes).
- **Card deck** — Shared deck that determines which tile all players must place each turn.
- **Sticker sheets** — 8 sheets of permanent stickers applied between episodes to modify boards.
- **Sealed envelopes** — 8 numbered envelopes (one per chapter) containing new rules, components, and objectives.
- **Score pad** — For tracking results between episodes.
- **Rulebook** — Base rules plus chapter-by-chapter legacy instructions.

#### Legacy Structure
- **24 episodes total** — 8 chapters with 3 episodes each.
- **Progressive modification** — After each episode, winning and losing players apply different stickers.
- **Permanent changes** — Stickers alter scoring rules and board features for all future episodes.
- **Envelope contents** — New mechanics, tokens, and rules introduced at the start of each chapter.

#### Eternal Game Mode
- **Post-campaign** — After completing all 24 episodes, boards are used for a standalone replayable game.
- **Board side B** — The reverse side of each board provides the eternal game layout.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
#### Personal Board Features
- **Landscape grid** — Rectangular grid of squares covering the board.
- **River** — A river runs through each board, dividing it into sections.
- **Rocks** — Grey squares that cost negative points if left uncovered at game end.
- **Trees** — Green squares that score positive points if left visible (in early episodes).
- **Light squares** — Open terrain easily covered by tiles.

#### Table Setup
1. **Distribute boards** — Each player takes one personal game board.
2. **Sort tile sets** — Each player arranges their identical set of polyomino tiles face-up for easy access.
3. **Shuffle card deck** — Prepare the shared building card deck.
4. **Open envelope if new chapter** — At the start of chapters 2-8, open the corresponding envelope and follow its instructions.
5. **Apply pre-episode stickers if applicable** — Some stickers are applied before the episode begins.

#### Simultaneous Play
- **No central board** — Each player builds on their own personal board.
- **Shared card reveals** — All players respond to the same card each turn.
- **No interaction** — Players do not affect each other's boards directly.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Per-Player Setup
- **Take board** — Each player takes their personal game board (with any accumulated stickers from prior episodes).
- **Take tile set** — Each player takes their complete set of polyomino building tiles.
- **Arrange tiles** — Lay tiles face-up in an organized manner for quick identification when cards are flipped.

#### Episode Preparation
- **Check chapter rules** — Ensure all players are aware of any new rules introduced by the current chapter's envelope.
- **Verify sticker state** — Confirm all players have applied the correct stickers from previous episodes.
- **Ready card deck** — Shuffle and place the building card deck centrally.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### Starting an Episode
1. **Flip first card** — Reveal the top card of the building deck showing a specific tile shape.
2. **All players simultaneously** — Find the matching tile from their set and place it on their board.
3. **Placement rules** — Tiles must be placed on empty squares; cannot overlap existing tiles or extend off the board.
4. **Continue flipping** — Proceed through the deck one card at a time.

#### First Episode Special Notes
- **Chapter 1, Episode 1** — Use base rules only; no stickers or envelope contents yet.
- **Scoring basics** — Negative points for visible rocks, positive points for visible trees.
- **Introduction** — Designed as a learning episode with straightforward scoring.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Turn Sequence
1. **Flip card** — Reveal the next building card from the shared deck.
2. **Identify tile** — All players find the matching polyomino tile in their personal set.
3. **Place tile** — Each player simultaneously places the tile on their board following placement rules.
4. **Pass option** — If you cannot or choose not to place the tile, you skip it (tile is set aside unused).
5. **Next card** — Flip the next card and repeat.

#### Placement Rules
- **Grid alignment** — Tiles must align with the grid squares on your board.
- **No overlap** — Tiles cannot be placed on top of other tiles.
- **No hanging off** — Tiles must fit entirely within the board boundaries.
- **Adjacency** — In most episodes, tiles must be placed adjacent to or connected to previously placed tiles (touching the river or existing tiles).
- **Orientation** — Tiles may be rotated and flipped freely.

#### Episode End
- **Deck depleted** — The episode ends when all cards have been flipped.
- **Alternatively** — Some episodes have special end conditions introduced by envelope rules.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Tile Placement Action
- **Mandatory identification** — When a card is flipped, you must use the exact tile shown.
- **Free placement** — You choose where on your board to place the tile (within placement rules).
- **Strategic covering** — Cover rocks to avoid negative points; avoid covering trees for positive points.

#### Pass Action
- **Voluntary skip** — You may choose not to place a tile even if legal placements exist.
- **Involuntary skip** — If no legal placement exists, you must skip.
- **No penalty for passing** — Skipped tiles are simply not placed (though missed coverage opportunities may cost points).

#### Legacy Actions (Between Episodes)
- **Winner stickers** — The episode winner applies specific reward stickers that improve their board.
- **Loser stickers** — Other players may apply catch-up stickers that provide different advantages.
- **Permanent modification** — Stickers change scoring zones, add bonus features, or alter board terrain.
- **Chapter transitions** — At new chapters, open the next envelope and integrate new rules and components.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Legacy Mechanic
- **Permanent changes** — Stickers applied to boards are permanent and affect all future episodes.
- **Catch-up mechanism** — Losing players often receive stickers that provide advantages, keeping the campaign competitive.
- **Branching experience** — Each player's board becomes unique over the campaign as different stickers accumulate.
- **No rewind** — Decisions and sticker placements cannot be undone.

#### Simultaneous Play
- **No downtime** — All players place tiles at the same time, keeping the game fast.
- **No direct interaction** — You cannot affect other players' boards.
- **Shared information** — Everyone sees the same card sequence and makes independent decisions.

#### Progressive Complexity
- **Chapter 1** — Basic tile placement and simple scoring.
- **Later chapters** — Envelopes introduce new tile types, scoring conditions, special terrain, and objective cards.
- **Escalating decisions** — Each chapter adds new considerations to tile placement choices.

#### Eternal Game Mode
- **Post-campaign play** — Use the B-side of boards for a standalone, non-legacy game.
- **Full ruleset** — Incorporates all mechanics learned during the campaign.
- **Replayable** — No further permanent modifications; play as many times as desired.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### Episode Scoring (Base Rules)
1. **Visible rocks** — Lose points for each rock square not covered by a tile (negative).
2. **Visible trees** — Gain points for each tree square still visible (positive in early episodes).
3. **Buildings completed** — Some building tiles score bonus points when fully placed in specific configurations.
4. **Legacy bonuses** — Points from sticker-modified zones and chapter-specific objectives.
5. **Total** — Sum all positives and negatives. Highest score wins the episode.

#### Campaign Scoring
- **Episode results** — Track wins and losses across all 24 episodes.
- **Sticker advantages** — Accumulated stickers create a personalized scoring landscape.
- **Overall winner** — Determined by cumulative performance across the campaign.

#### Tiebreaker
- **Fewer visible rocks** — Player with fewer uncovered rocks wins ties.
- **Further tie** — Share the victory.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
#### Early Placement Strategy
- **Cover rocks first** — Prioritize placing tiles over rock squares to eliminate negative points.
- **Protect trees** — Plan tile placement to leave tree squares visible for positive points.
- **Build from the river** — Use the river as an anchor point and expand outward systematically.
- **Leave flexible gaps** — Do not create isolated pockets that only specific tile shapes can fill.

#### Campaign Awareness
- **Read ahead minimally** — Understand current chapter rules thoroughly before placing tiles.
- **Sticker placement matters** — Choose sticker locations carefully, as they affect all future episodes.
- **Adapt to new rules** — Each chapter shifts priorities; be ready to change strategy.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
#### Mistake 1 — Ignoring Tile Order Probability
- **Why it fails** — Placing tiles without considering which tiles are likely to come next creates unfillable gaps.
- **Fix** — Know your tile set and anticipate upcoming shapes.

#### Mistake 2 — Covering Trees Carelessly
- **Why it fails** — Trees are often worth positive points; covering them unnecessarily costs points.
- **Fix** — Route tiles around tree squares whenever possible.

#### Mistake 3 — Creating Isolated Gaps
- **Why it fails** — Small isolated empty areas may not match any remaining tile shapes.
- **Fix** — Keep open areas connected and large enough for multiple tile options.

#### Mistake 4 — Poor Sticker Placement
- **Why it fails** — Stickers are permanent; placing them in suboptimal locations weakens future episodes.
- **Fix** — Consider long-term board state, not just immediate benefit.

#### Mistake 5 — Not Adapting to Chapter Rules
- **Why it fails** — New chapters change scoring priorities; old strategies may become suboptimal.
- **Fix** — Re-evaluate priorities at the start of each new chapter.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### Decision 1 — Cover Rocks vs Preserve Trees
- **Rock focus** — Eliminates guaranteed negative points.
- **Tree focus** — Preserves guaranteed positive points.
- **Balance** — Optimal play usually covers rocks while routing around trees.

#### Decision 2 — Tight Packing vs Flexible Gaps
- **Tight packing** — Maximizes coverage but risks creating unfillable spaces.
- **Flexible gaps** — Leaves room for awkward tile shapes but may leave more rocks exposed.

#### Decision 3 — Sticker Placement (Legacy)
- **Offensive stickers** — Place on high-value scoring zones for maximum future benefit.
- **Defensive stickers** — Place to neutralize problematic board areas.

#### Decision 4 — Pass or Place
- **Place** — Even suboptimal placement may cover a rock or maintain adjacency.
- **Pass** — Sometimes preserving board state for a better future tile is worth skipping.
